Summary: Set of complementary tools (Compass, Guide, Toolkit) for designing AI-mediated learning experiences responsibly.
ASU's foundational ethical-design lens that underpins the AI-X tools.
20–25 minute guided self-assessment to clarify use case, examine fit, surface risks, and get next-step recommendation for an AI-X idea.
Self-assessment of a working prototype across 8 themes using Implemented/Partially/Not Yet/N/A levels.
Menu of tools across four design stages: Challenge, Creation, Implementation, Investigation.
Define learning objectives, learners, and conditions before jumping to a solution.
Iterative root-cause questioning to find the underlying educational problem.
Decompose what learners must do to identify cognitive and procedural steps.
Build representative learner personas and scenarios to ground design decisions.
Surface needs and values of all impacted stakeholders.
Plain-language explanation of how AI systems function to inform appropriate use.
Structured ethical evaluation of an AI tool idea before building.
Orientation for design and development of an AI tool.
Ground design decisions in learning-sciences research.
Build low/high-fidelity prototypes to test design intent.
Capture functional and non-functional requirements for the tool.
Map inputs → activities → outputs → outcomes → impact for the AI tool.
Plan what data the tool collects, stores, and uses—including privacy.
Orientation for testing the tool in real conditions.
Practical guidance for making AI tools accessible.
Methods for testing usability with intended users.
Orientation for analyzing data collected about the tool.
Comparison of methods for collecting tool-effectiveness data.
Compare tool variants with controlled experiments.
Design and deploy surveys to gather user feedback.
Qualitative methods for deeper user insight.
